# {{REPO_NAME}}

> {{DESCRIPTION}} — a **Bun + Effect v4** workspace with source-driven agent skills and GitHub Actions, powered by **skill-gen** and **workflow-gen**.

---

## What is this?

`{{REPO_NAME}}` is a Bun monorepo on **Effect v4**. It is intentionally lean: no
build step, no bundler, no global installs. Bun runs the TypeScript directly,
Effect v4 powers the services and runtime surfaces, and everything is gated
behind a single `bun run ci`.

Derived files (agent skills and CI workflows) are generated from fragments
embedded next to the code they describe, so docs and pipelines stay in sync with
the source.

## Layout

```
.agents/
  skills/      Agent skills (hand-written + generated by skill-gen)
  commands/    Slash-command definitions
.github/
  actions/     Shared GitHub Actions setup
  workflows/   Generated by workflow-gen
packages/
  ci/skill-gen/               Skill-file generator (Bun + Effect v4 CLI)
  ci/workflow-gen/            GitHub Actions workflow generator
  ci/actions/                 Co-located CI checkers
  tooling/typescript-config/  Shared tsconfig presets (base.json, bun.json)
  {{SCOPE}}/{{PACKAGE}}/      Core domain package (Effect v4 services)
apps/                         Runtime services (HTTP API, workers, …)
vendor/
  effect-smol/                Effect v4 source — git submodule, reference only
```

## Getting started

This repo vendors the Effect v4 source as a submodule, so clone recursively:

```bash
git clone --recurse-submodules <repo-url>
cd {{REPO_NAME}}
bun install
```

Already cloned without submodules? Run `git submodule update --init`.

## Development

```bash
bun run ci          # biome + typecheck + vitest + knip + generator drift checks
bun run check       # Biome (format + lint)
bun run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit, per package
bun run test        # Vitest
bun run knip        # unused-code analysis
```

Conventions for agents and contributors live in [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md)
(symlinked as `CLAUDE.md`) and the skills under `.agents/skills/`.
